modules.title: Skin care for the whole you

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Our whole-body, integrative approach for skin care
What your care may include:
✓ Medical & Aesthetic Skin Treatments
From acne treatments to Botox, peels, and microneedling, Tia offers evidence based options to heal, smooth and refresh your skin
✓ A plan that fits your life
Care built around your health, body, and goals, so your skin routine actually works for you
✓ Safety you can trust
Licensed MDs, NPs, and PAs deliver every treatment at Tia, combining expertise with a whole health lens
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted

testimonials.Content: I’ve never experienced such personalized, sensitive, thorough, and holistic women’s healthcare in my life. So grateful for this clinic!
testimonials.Title: Whitney K, Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: I can’t recommend Tia enough. As a woman who doesn’t like going to the doctor (white coat syndrome) and has been discounted and not listened to by doctors in the past, this experience is everything a doctors appt should be. I am so happy I am now a Tia patient and would highly recommend Tia to any female friend or family member that could benefit from the amazing staff and care here 💕
testimonials.Title: Katherine E., Tia Patient

testimonials.Content: Tia has totally changed my life! I’ve never had such great access to healthcare until I discovered Tia. It’s so easy to make an appointment, they offer everything I could want, and everyone is incredibly kind. Grecia made me feel super comfortable with the entire experience. I couldn’t rate Tia more highly :)
testimonials.Title: Karen C. Tia Patient

children.text: Birth Control - Contraception Counseling
Your First Step to Finding the Right Birth Control

items: Share your goals, preferences, and history
Talk through all your options, including the pill, IUD, patch, ring, implant, and more.
Get a prescription or plan for your next steps

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
We listen to your needs
Your provider will listen to your current symptoms, concerns, and goals to understand the full picture of your health.
We review your health history
We'll go over your medical, birth control and period history to help inform your options.
We build a personalized contraception plan

We build a personalized contraception plan. Your provider will partner with you to explore a full range of options, including cycle tracking, the pill, IUD, patch, ring, or implant, to find the best fit for your body, lifestyle, and goals.
Who should have a Birth Control Consult?
You should book this appointment if you're looking to start a new birth control method, need to renew a current prescription, or want to switch to a different type of contraception.
Symptoms we care for
If you have never taken birth control before (or simply took a break from taking it), there are several reasons and symptoms that might make you a good candidate for contraception:
Pregnancy prevention
Irregular periods
Heavy or painful periods
Missed or skipped periods
Managing the symptoms of PCOS, like acne or hair growth
How the Birth Control appointment can help
This appointment can help you:
Make an informed decision:
Get clarity on the pros, cons, and effectiveness of different birth control methods, so you can choose what feels right for you.
Get a clear roadmap:
Create a practical plan for your next steps, whether that's a prescription for the pill, a separate appointment for an IUD insertion, or guidance on non-hormonal options.
Explore a wide range of options:
Learn about hormonal and non-hormonal contraception, as well as the latest medical advancements in the field

cells: Method
How It Works - Effectiveness
Pros & Cons
The Implant (Nexplanon)

Your provider places it under the skin of your upper arm. It has to be removed by your provider.

Effectiveness: >99%

Pros: Good for five years. You may not have a period at all.

Cons:It is possible to bleed for longer than desired amounts of time and the bleeding can be unpredictable.
IUD (Paragard, Mirena, Kyleena, Skyla)

Must be placed in the uterus by your provider. Your provider also needs to remove it.

Effectiveness: >99%

Pros: Effective for up to 12 years depending on which IUD you choose. Hormonal IUDs can decrease or stop bleeding. The copper IUD does not have any hormones.

Cons: You need an appt to get the IUD inserted and removed. Paragard, the 12-year IUD, can increase bleeding.
The Patch (Xulane, Twirla)

Apply a new patch once a week for three weeks. No patch needed for week four.

Effectiveness: >93%

Pros: No need to take a pill every single day.

Cons: Depending where it’s placed on the body, it can be visible. Not ideal for swimmers or those who have increased sweating.
The Ring (NuvaRing, Annovera)

Insert a small ring into the vagina each month or annually depending on the ring of choice.

Effectiveness: >93%

Pros: Easy to start and stop when desired.

Cons: You need to remove and replace the ring. Some might want to remove it for sex, though it can stay in the vagina during sex.
Depo Shot (Depo Provera)

Your provider will give you a shot every three months (13 weeks).

Effectiveness: >96%

Pros: You only need to think about it every three months.

Cons: It’s a shot! You need to come to the clinic every three months for it.
The Pill

Daily pill.

Effectiveness: >93%

Pros: You can control your bleeding by continuously cycling. You can start and stop the pill easily.

Cons: You need to remember to take it every day.

children.text: Your Birth Control Appointment: What to Expect
Preparing for Your Appointment
Before your appointment, we recommend updating your medical history, including any past or current medications. Think about your goals for contraception and any questions you have.
During Your Appointment

You’ll meet with one of Tia’s providers who is well versed in contraception options. Your provider will review your medical history, period history, medication history, and contraception goals. You'll also have a discussion about the pros, cons, and effectiveness of each birth control option, as well as potential risks or side effects.
After Your Appointment
You’ll leave your appointment with a clear plan for next steps. These may include:
Prescription:
If you choose a method like the pill, patch, ring, or shot, your provider can send a prescription to your pharmacy.
Procedure:
If you and your provider decide on an IUD or implant, your Care Coordinator will help you schedule a separate in-person appointment for the placement.
Continued Support:
If you have any questions, your care team will be available to support.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! We will bill your insurance for this visit.
Our experts in Birth Control
Where to get an appointment
Book an appointment

Choosing the right birth control is an important part of caring for your health and planning your future. A birth control counseling appointment helps you explore your options, from pills and patches to long-acting methods like IUDs and implants, so you can find what fits your body, your lifestyle, and your goals with personalized guidance from your care team.

children.text: Yearly Skin Exam

items: A head-to-toe skin check with a specially trained Tia primary care provider
A plan for next steps if we find something that needs attention, from monitoring to treatment or referral.
Personalized guidance on protecting your skin and performing self-checks at home to support early detection

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
A Yearly Skin Exam is an in-clinic visit with a specially trained Tia primary care provider to check your skin from head to toe, spot any changes early, and support your long-term skin health.
Who should get a Yearly Skin Exam?

A Yearly Skin Exam is for anyone who wants to take care of their skin and catch changes early. Regular checks are one of the best ways to prevent skin cancer from going undetected. This visit may be especially important if you:
Have fair skin, freckles, or a history of sunburns or tanning
Notice new or changing moles, spots, or patches on your skin
Have a family history of basal cell carcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma or precancerous growths
Spend a lot of time outdoors or have high sun exposure
Symptoms we care for:
During your Yearly Skin Exam, your provider will pay close attention to skin changes such as:
New or unusual moles, spots, or patches
Growths that are changing in size, shape, or color
Lesions that are itchy, painful, bleeding, or not healing
Rough, scaly patches (which can be precancerous)
How the Yearly Skin Exam can help:
Early detection:
Spot skin changes early, when they’re easiest to treat
Comprehensive evaluation:
Receive a thorough, head-to-toe exam from a trained provider
Personalized guidance:
Learn how to protect your skin and check for changes at home
Next steps if needed:
Access follow-up care, such as a biopsy or dermatologist referral
Your Yearly Skin Exam: What to Expect
Step 1
Arrive for your in-clinic exam with a Tia primary care provider (MD, NP, or PA).
Step 2

Get a thorough head-to-toe skin check - including scalp, face, neck, arms, chest, back, abdomen, legs, feet, and even between your toes. If your provider finds anything they want to get more information about, they may recommend a biopsy (sometimes done on the spot) or a referral to a dermatologist.
Step 3
Leave with personalized education on self-checks and skin protection. Plan to return yearly (or sooner if you notice new or changing spots).
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?

Tia will submit this visit to your insurance.

It’s important to know that many plans don’t treat yearly skin exams as preventive care unless you have a specific concern. When that happens, the visit is billed like a regular office visit — which may mean a copay or some out-of-pocket costs depending on your plan. 

We recommend checking your specific benefits ahead of your visit.
Our experts in Yearly Skin Exams
Primary Care, Holistic Medicine, Wellness Promotion

Ashley is a Family Nurse Practitioner with experience serving a wide population of patients across multiple specialties including family practice, cardiology and urgent care. She enjoys taking the time to work in tandem with her patients to create a fully informed and well-rounded approach to their healthcare plans. She is passionate about empowering women to become well educated advocates for their own health and well-being, and feels it is a gift and a privilege to be a part of that process.

She takes a compassionate patient-centered approach to care and believes in listening first to uncover family systems. Fluent in English and Spanish, her goal as a provider is to do as much as possible for her patients to help them feel calm and confident.
Trauma-informed care, management of complex chronic disease, patient empowerment

children.text: People living with HIV, caring for those who feel stigmatized by the healthcare system, sexual health

main.position: Market Medical Director, Los Angeles, She/her

children.text: I began my health care career in the heat of the AIDS epidemic. I had no intention of working in medicine but was unable to sit and watch. I needed to do something. So my career began. I have had the privilege of caring for folks living with HIV for 30 years. I have had the honor of working with amazing teams of committed people who share my vision. Additionally I have spent the last 10 years working in Uganda building a health system for those living in a remote area of Western Uganda. What a gift it has been.

Strongly believes that time spent with a patient is done in a judgement free zone.

children.text: About Tia San Francisco

Located conveniently at the intersection of Mission and Van Ness Ave. Designed to make the female healthcare experience more welcoming and comforting, Tia members can enjoy an open layout waiting room with specialty beverages and new books to browse through.

cells: Where to find us
Our address is 1500 Mission Street. Enter the front door of the building on Mission St, below the Tia sign. Ring the doorbell to your left.
Where to park

SF Mission offers parking validation for the parking garage at 90 11th Street, San Francisco CA. The price starts at $10/hour but should not exceed $20 total with parking validation from the clinic. If garage has sign stating full, please disregard and proceed to park there as we have reserved spot for Tia patients.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

children.text: No, you will be seeing a Tia primary care provider (a Nurse Practitioner, Physician's Assistant, or Doctor) who has received specialized training in dermatology. They are equipped to handle many skin concerns. If your condition requires the care of a board-certified dermatologist, our team will provide a referral.

faqs.question: Will I be seeing a board-certified dermatologist for my Yearly Skin Exam?

children.text: A yearly skin exam is a proactive way to protect your skin health. It’s particularly important if you have fair skin, a history of sunburns or tanning, many or changing moles, or a family history of skin cancer.
If you have a personal history of skin cancer, we recommend scheduling your ongoing skin checks with a dermatologist rather than with a Tia primary care provider.

faqs.question: Who should get a yearly skin exam?

children.text: You can use your insurance for this visit. However, some insurance plans may not cover an yearly skin exam as preventive care. This means you may need to pay a copay or other out-of-pocket costs. We recommend calling your insurance to confirm whether they cover a full-body skin exam as a preventive service.

faqs.question: How do I know if this will be covered by my insurance?

children.text: Book an appointment

Taking care of your skin is key to staying healthy and catching changes early.
A yearly skin exam helps you check for moles, spots, or growths that may need attention.
Your provider will examine your skin from head to toe, answer your questions, and help you understand what’s normal and what’s not, so you can feel confident about your skin’s health year-round.

main.shortDescription: A head-to-toe skin check where your primary care provider examines your moles, spots, and lesions. If something needs a closer look, we’ll guide you through next steps like referral or biopsy. You’ll leave with clear guidance on how to protect your skin every day.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s primary care services may be right for you if you:
Need a primary care provider or are overdue for an annual exam
Have a common illness like a cough, cold, flu, or a UTI.
Experiencing headaches, body aches, or unexplained pain.
Need support managing a chronic condition like high blood pressure or diabetes.
Feel anxious, overwhelmed, or "off," and you're not sure why.
Need a prescription refilled or want to discuss medication management.
Have ongoing issues like gut problems or trouble sleeping.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

modules.title: Skin care for the whole you

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Our whole-body, integrative approach for skin care

✓ Medical & Aesthetic Skin Treatments
From acne treatments to Botox, peels, and microneedling, Tia offers evidence based options to heal, smooth and refresh your skin
✓ A plan that fits your life
Care built around your health, body, and goals, so your skin routine actually works for you
✓ Safety you can trust
Licensed MDs, NPs, and PAs deliver every treatment at Tia, combining expertise with a whole health lens

children.text: What is a skin care appointment?
An acne, rash, or skin change visit at Tia can be facilitated in clinic or virtually and is conducted by an MD or nurse pracitioner.
When should you schedule a skin care appointment?
It is recommended to schedule an appointment to discuss skin changes if:
Your symptoms are not responding to over the counter treatment.
Your skin changes are associated with the menstrual cycle.
Your symptoms are becoming bothersome or are worsening over time.
What to expect during a skin care appointment
Here’s what you can expect during a Tia dermatology appointment:
A comprehensive assessment:
Your provider will factor in your skin concerns, overall health, and well-being to create your personalized treatment plan.
A customized Care Plan:

Based on your assessment, your provider will work with you to develop a personalized treatment plan. This may include a combination of medications, lifestyle recommendations, holistic therapies like acupuncture, or other interventions aimed at optimizing your skin health.
Follow-up care:
Seeing changes in your skin can take time, and our team will be there every step of the way to make sure your Care Plan is working.

After your appointment, we'll provide clear guidance on next steps. Whether it's booking a follow-up, adjusting your treatment plan, or providing ongoing support, we'll ensure you get the comprehensive care you deserve.
Emotional support:

Skin concerns can bring up a lot of feelings, and we're here to listen, provide support, and put you at ease. At Tia, care is collaborative. You’re always welcome to ask questions, express concerns, or discuss any care preferences with your provider.
Potential treatment plans for skin issues
Once your provider has conducted a thorough medical exam and reviewed your medical history associated with your skin issues, there are several treatment options they might recommend:
Depending on your symptoms, your provider might recommend additional lab testing.
Your provider might recommened keeping a symptom journal to track when, how often, and for how long your symptoms appear.
Depending on symptoms and severity, ertain over the counter medication can be recommended or your provider will perscribe medication.
If your provider suspects that your symptoms are more severe, you might recieve a referral to a dermatology specialist.
Got skin issues? Join Tia and schedule an appointment virtualy or at the clinic nearest you!
Located in New York, Scottsdale, San Francisco, and Los Angeles.

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

We’ll take the time to understand your skin concerns, talk through your options, and build a care plan just for you.
Who Can Benefit from a Skin Care Consultation? Here’s What to Look For
You’re dealing with acne scars, sensitivity, dryness, or signs of aging
You’re considering treatments like chemical peels or botox and want expert guidance
You’re unsure which products—like retinoids, serums, or moisturizers—are right for your skin
You want a skin care plan that’s tailored to your goals and overall health
A simple plan for real results, inside and out
Step 1
Your Aesthetic Nurse Practitioner will work to understand your skin concerns and aesthetic goals.
Step 2
You’ll receive expert guidance on any topics you are curious about – ideal treatments for your goals, the perfect skin care regimen, and more.
Step 3
If applicable, your provider will help you book any treatments or procedures that are right for you.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
This is a cosmetic service and therefore not covered by insurance.
If not, what is out of pocket cost?
The consultation is available at a flat rate of $49.

modules.title: Skin care for the whole you

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Our whole-body, integrative approach for skin care

children.text: Clinical Wrinkle Care

Feel comfortable in the skin you’re in. Experience the welcoming environment of a Tia clinic and work with a highly trained Tia provider on a customized injection plan using products like Botox or Xeomin to help you feel like your best you.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is Clinical Wrinkle Care at Tia?
Clinical Wrinkle Care is the treatment of wrinkles and signs of aging skin through injectables like Botox and Xeomin, delivered by Tia’s licensed advanced practice clinicians (NPs & PAs).

These treatments work by temporarily relaxing the muscles that cause lines and wrinkles from repeated facial expressions—like frowning, squinting, or raising your eyebrows. This allows us to target areas in both the upper face (forehead lines, frown lines, eyebrow lift, crow's feet, bunny lines) and lower face (lip lines, masseters, lip flip, smile lift, dimpled chin).
With a whole-health approach, your provider creates a personalized plan to deliver natural-looking results that help you feel your best.
Who Can Benefit from Clinical Wrinkle Care? Here’s What to Look For
You’re noticing fine lines or wrinkles from repeated facial expressions
You want to smooth existing wrinkles or prevent new ones from forming
You’re looking for natural-looking results that still feel like you
You want care that considers your overall health, not just your skin

With a robust background in emergency medicine and a dedication to addressing chronic disease, I pursued advanced education to become a Nurse Practitioner specializing in primary care, with a focus on functional lifestyle, regenerative, and longevity medicine. My passion is centered on empowering women to seize control of their health through comprehensive education on their bodies and personalized wellness plans, aimed at fostering longevity and preventing disease. Through my practice, I aim to cultivate a supportive environment where patients can take charge of their health and realize a life filled with vitality and fulfillment.

1. Undetectable injectables.
2. Managing trickier anatomy like hooded eyelids or jowls.
3. Therapeutic neuromodulator injections, beyond aesthetic indications.

Alexa is a nurse practitioner over a decade of experience in primary care which has given her a special appreciation for holistic care and health optimization. Honing in on the medical aesthetics field, Alexa goes above and beyond to make sure each patient assessment is individualized, and treatment is directed by differences in anatomy and desired outcomes. When providing aesthetic injections and procedures, Alexa's confident demeanor and gentle touch puts patients at ease. To her, medical aesthetics empowers individuals to look and feel their best and there's nothing that Alexa loves more than giving patients a little more strut in their step after they leave her treatment chair.

Located conveniently at the intersection of Mission and Van Ness Ave. Designed to make the female healthcare experience more welcoming and comforting, Tia members can enjoy an open layout waiting room with specialty beverages and new books to browse through.

cells: Where to find us
Our address is 1500 Mission Street. Enter the front door of the building on Mission St, below the Tia sign. Ring the doorbell to your left.
Where to park

SF Mission offers parking validation for the parking garage at 90 11th Street, San Francisco CA. The price starts at $10/hour but should not exceed $20 total with parking validation from the clinic. If garage has sign stating full, please disregard and proceed to park there as we have reserved spot for Tia patients.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

Have an active infection at the injection site
Are taking certain medications like blood thinners or muscle relaxants

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

Dealing with stomach pain, bloating, nausea, or ongoing discomfort? A visit for stomach pain issues helps identify what’s causing your symptoms and builds a personalized plan to ease pain, support digestion, and help you feel like yourself again.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

Have a common illness like a cough, cold, flu, or a UTI.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

modules.title: Skin care for the whole you

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted

children.text: Skin Procedure

items: Treat specific skin concerns with an in-clinic procedure performed by a specially trained Tia primary care provider
Remove and test a small skin sample with a skin biopsy, or freeze and treat abnormal tissue with cryotherapy
Leave with a personalized care plan, clear aftercare instructions, and next steps for your skin health

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is this appointment?
An in-clinic visit for minor skin procedures, recommended when your provider sees a spot, mole, lesion, wart, or other growth that needs closer evaluation or treatment.
The procedure may include a biopsy (removing a small sample for lab testing) or cryotherapy (freezing abnormal tissue with liquid nitrogen)
This visit is focused on giving you answers, relief, and a clear plan for next steps in your skin health
Who should get a Skin Procedure?

This visit is for patients who have seen a Tia primary care provider for a skin concern and were recommended a procedure like a biopsy, cryotherapy, or treatment of a spot, lesion, wart, or precancerous growth.
Symptoms we care for
Following an evaluation of your skin, your Tia provider may recommend a Skin Procedure to address concerns like:
Suspicious moles or growths
Precancerous or abnormal lesions
Painful or bothersome warts or other skin changes
If your care requires more advanced procedures or specialized follow-up, your Tia provider will refer you to a dermatologist.
How the Skin Procedure can help:
Provides answers by testing suspicious spots for skin cancer or other conditions
Removes or treats bothersome warts, precancerous lesions, or other abnormal tissue quickly in-clinic
Ensures you leave with a personalized care plan and clear next steps for your skin health
Your Skin Procedure Appointment: What to Expect
Preparing for your appointment
Review your care plan to understand the next steps your Tia provider recommended for your skin procedure. No additional prep is needed.
During your appointment

This in-clinic procedure is usually about 30 minutes. Your Tia provider will explain the procedure and answer questions before starting. Depending on your specific skin concern, the visit may include a biopsy (numbing the area and removing a small sample for testing) or cryotherapy (using liquid nitrogen to freeze and treat abnormal tissue).ood.
After your appointment
Procedures usually require little to no downtime. Your provider will share aftercare guidance with you to help you care for the treated area at home.
If you had a biopsy, your provider will send your sample to the lab and will share results and next steps once they’re available (usually in 1-2 weeks).
Your care plan will be updated to reflect follow-up recommendations (which may include repeat treatment or referral to a dermatologist) and ongoing skin health support.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
Yes! Tia will bill your insurance for your visit. Just like at any doctor’s office, you may be responsible for a co-pay or coinsurance, depending on your health plan.

If you have a biopsy, the sample is sent to Quest Diagnostics. Quest will bill your insurance directly for the lab testing, and you may receive a bill for any remaining balance depending on your coverage.
Our experts in Skin Procedure

Alexandra is a board certified family nurse practitioner with 12 years of clinical experience in the ICU, family medicine, and urology. She has a passion for women’s health with the goal of facilitating a healthcare experience that is comprehensive and educational. Alexandra believes that optimal patient health and wellness is achieved through shared decision-making and a trusting partnership.
Depression/anxiety, women's health, acute care

Bianca Villar is a bilingual Nurse Practitioner with over 6 years of experience and an expertise in women’s mental health. She takes a compassionate patient-centered approach to care and believes in listening first to uncover family systems. Fluent in English and Spanish, her goal as a provider is to do as much as possible for her patients to help them feel calm and confident.
Abortion, Gender-Affirming Hormone Treatment, and PMDD

Chelsey is an experienced provider in sexual and reproductive health who likes to focus on each patients needs while providing high quality care. Identifying on the LGBTQ+ spectrum, she enjoys speaking with patients about birth control options, their specific sex practices, and is experienced in providing gender affirming care for patients who identify on the gender spectrum. She loves to run, eat candy, and watch all the trending television shows.

children.text: About Tia San Francisco

Located conveniently at the intersection of Mission and Van Ness Ave. Designed to make the female healthcare experience more welcoming and comforting, Tia members can enjoy an open layout waiting room with specialty beverages and new books to browse through.

cells: Where to find us
Our address is 1500 Mission Street. Enter the front door of the building on Mission St, below the Tia sign. Ring the doorbell to your left.
Where to park

SF Mission offers parking validation for the parking garage at 90 11th Street, San Francisco CA. The price starts at $10/hour but should not exceed $20 total with parking validation from the clinic. If garage has sign stating full, please disregard and proceed to park there as we have reserved spot for Tia patients.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

children.text: Microneedling

Improve skin texture, reduce scarring, and even out skin tone by stimulating collagen with microneedling. At Tia, we use the SkinPen® device, and highly trained providers who will help you reach your unique skin health goals. Regular sessions can help you achieve smoother, more even-toned skin.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book
Already a Tia member? Book here
How does microneedling work?

Microneedling uses ultra-fine needles to create hundreds to thousands of precise micro-injuries in the skin, triggering the body’s natural healing response. This process stimulates collagen and elastin production, promotes tissue remodeling without scarring, and delivers long-lasting rejuvenation. Most patients can resume normal activities within 24 hours.
What is SkinPen microneedling at Tia?

Microneedling with SkinPen is a cutting-edge skin rejuvenation treatment designed to refine skin texture, minimize wrinkles, reduce pore size, and diminish the appearance of scars. At Tia, we use the advanced SkinPen technology to stimulate collagen production and accelerate skin cell turnover, revealing a smoother, firmer, and more radiant complexion.
What can microneedling help with?
Microneedling can help address common skin concerns such as:
Acne scarring
Surgical scars
Enlarged pores
Wrinkles
Stretch marks
Signs of aging
Dull skin
Sun damage
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?
This is a cosmetic service and therefore not covered by insurance. See out of pocket costs below.

children.text: Microneedling: What to expect
Step 1

Patients arrive 30 minutes before their appointment to numb the treatment area. Your provider will review your skin goals, explain the procedure, cleanse your skin, and apply a topical numbing cream to ensure comfort.
Step 2

Once the numbing cream has taken effect, your provider will begin the microneedling treatment. Using the SkinPen device, they’ll gently glide over your skin for about 15 minutes. Most patients say it feels like a light vibration or gentle scratching.
Step 3

When the microneedling is finished, your provider will apply soothing serums or creams to help calm your skin. It’s common to notice some redness, warmth, or mild swelling (like a sunburn) for the first day or two.
Most patients resume normal activities right away, with any redness typically fading within 24–48 hours.
Our experts in Microneedling

modules.title: Where to get a Microneedling Procedure

children.text: While microneedling is safe, there are certain situations in which a patient should not receive this treatment. This includes patients who:
Have active skin cancer in the treatment area(s)
Have open wounds, sores, or irritated skin in the treatment area(s)
Are prone to keloid scarring
Are allergic to stainless steel or anesthetics
Have a bleeding disorder or dysfunction
Are pregnant or nursing
Are taking drugs with the ingredient isotretinoin (such as Accutane)

faqs.question: Who is not a good fit for this appointment?

children.text: Tia uses specialty trained licensed advanced practice clinicians (NPs & PAs) to deliver this care.

faqs.question: Who delivers this care?

children.text: The duration of your results depends on many factors, which you and your provider will discuss. For acne scarring and signs of aging, the most common treatment cadence is 3 treatments, spaced 1 month apart. After this initial treatment window, most patients return for a treatment once every 6 months or 1 year.

faqs.question: How long do microneedling results last?

children.text: SkinPen is the first FDA-cleared microneedling device. It’s proven safe and effective, delivering results that last up to six months. It’s a quick and easy treatment with little to no recovery period. SkinPen is proven to improve the appearance of acne scars and wrinkles on the neck. It doesn’t rely on heat or chemicals, so the results are safe for all skin tones and types.

faqs.question: Why did Tia select SkinPen as a device?

At the start of every visit, you and your provider will align on your goals and your treatment plan, with you always in the driver’s seat.

modules.title: Skin care for the whole you

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Our whole-body, integrative approach for skin care

From acne treatments to Botox, peels, and microneedling, Tia offers evidence based options to heal, smooth and refresh your skin
✓ A plan that fits your life

✓ Safety you can trust

children.text: Chemical Peel
Improve the texture and tone of your skin, reduce fine lines, and treat acne scars or discoloration with a medical-grade chemical peel, tailored to your skin type and goals.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book
Already a Tia member? Book here
What is a chemical peel and how does it work?

A chemical peel is a skin treatment where a chemical solution exfoliates the outer layers of skin, promoting new cell growth. Peels vary in depth (superficial, medium, or deep) and can be customized to your skin concerns.
Which peels does Tia offer?

Tia carefully selected a set of chemical peels to address a variety of skin care concerns on both the face and body. We use VI Chemical Peels because of their effectiveness, applicability across a wide range of skin concerns, comfort, and aftercare support.
VI Chemical Peel
Peel used: VI Peel Original
Improves skin texture, reduces fine lines, brightens tone; gentle yet effective
Good for signs of aging, rough texture, rosacea, first-time peelers, and sensitive skin
VI Chemical Peel Comprehensive
Peel used: VI Peel Precision Plus or VI Peel Purify with Precision Plus
Targets pigment and acne; reveals clearer, more even-toned skin
Good for sun damage, stubborn dark spots, melasma, acne, post-breakout marks, excessive oil production, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation
VI Chemical Peel for Body
Peel used: VI Body Peel
Smooths and renews body skin; fades discoloration and improves texture.
Good for body acne, pigmentation, scars, stretch marks. Suitable for most body parts and safe for all skin tones.

main.shortDescription: Improve skin texture, reduce fine lines, and reveal a brighter, more even tone with a medical-grade VI Peel. This effective yet gentle peel is great for first-timer peelers and sensitive skin.
main.title: VI Chemical Peel

main.shortDescription: Our most powerful medical-grade peel, tailored to your skin goals. We’ll use VI Peel Precision Plus to lift stubborn dark spots & melasma, or VI Peel Purify with Precision Plus to calm acne and fade post-breakout marks - unveiling a clearer, brighter complexion fast.

main.title: VI Chemical Peel for Body

children.text: Who is it for?

Chemical peels are great if you’re looking for a gentle and customizable medical-grade treatment to refresh your skin, target stubborn discoloration, or support acne-prone skin. VI Peels are safe for most skin types, including skin of color.
What can chemical peels help with?
Chemical peels can help address common skin concerns on both the face and body:
Uneven tone and texture
Fine lines and early signs of aging
Acne and acne scarring
Hyperpigmentation, melasma, or sun damage
Dull or congested skin.
Insurance and Pricing
Is this covered by insurance?

Chemical Peel: What to Expect
Step 1
Each treatment lasts around 15-20 minutes and begins with your provider reviewing your skin care needs and any relevant health history.
Step 2
Based on your goals and skin type, your provider will select a chemical peel formula and will apply the peel in layers.
Step 3

Your provider will give you a take-home kit with post-treatment instructions and skin care products for after-care. You may notice some flaking by day 2 and peeling by days 3–5. By day 6, the peeling will ease; by day 7, most patients have stopped peeling.

Where to park

SF Mission offers parking validation for the parking garage at 90 11th Street, San Francisco CA. The price starts at $10/hour but should not exceed $20 total with parking validation from the clinic. If garage has sign stating full, please disregard and proceed to park there as we have reserved spot for Tia patients.

Why go to multiple doctors’ offices—primary care clinic here, gynecologist there—when you can have everything you need at one women’s health clinic in New York City? At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.

With locations in the Manhattan (Soho) and Brooklyn (Williamsburg), we’re making it easier for women across New York City to access personalized care that prioritizes your health. Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.

Are pregnant or breastfeeding
